title: Audit + fix duplicate XP grant on strike-completes-duel
description:
# Duplicate XP grant when strike defeats AND completes duel simultaneously

## Symptom

`CmdStrike._defeat` calls `attacker.gain_experience(EXP_ON_VICTORY)` (5 XP).
`handle_duel_strike` (from e17.4 PR #73) ALSO calls
`winner.gain_experience(EXP_ON_VICTORY + BONUS_XP_ON_WIN)` (5 + 10 = 15 XP).

If a single strike both reduces target integrity to 0 AND hits the
duel-score threshold (3 strikes), the winner receives **20 XP** instead of
the intended 15.

Existing tests avoid this by setting target integrity to 1000, so the bug is
latent — not exercised — but real.

## Fix

Cleanest: remove the `attacker.gain_experience(EXP_ON_VICTORY)` call from
`CmdStrike._defeat` and rely solely on the duel-end hook. This requires
verifying the strike path outside duels (PvE strikes against daemons) still
grants XP somewhere — the e19.4 EXP curve work (in flight as
`gridwars_run-obh`) will likely wire PvE XP via a different path
(`gridwars/world/exp_curve.py`), so the `_defeat` call may already be
redundant after that lands.

Wait for e17.4 (PR #73) AND e19.4 to both land, then audit XP grants
holistically.

## Acceptance

1. PvP duel-end grants exactly `EXP_ON_VICTORY + BONUS_XP_ON_WIN` XP, never more.
2. PvE daemon kill grants XP per the e19.4 curve, never doubled.
3. PvP non-duel strike (if such a thing exists) behaves consistently.
4. Test added: a duel where the killing strike both reaches integrity 0 and
   triggers 3-strike threshold asserts winner XP = 15.

## Depends on

e17.4 (PR #73) AND e19.4 (in flight `gridwars_run-obh`) both merging first.

## Codex vs Claude

Claude — combat-path audit + targeted test.

## Skills

andrej-karpathy-skills:karpathy-guidelines, source-driven-development,
test-driven-development.

title: YAS: scale SOFT_LIMIT in build_wide/medium/narrow cost-row mini-bars
description:
# YAS: hardcoded SOFT_LIMIT in build_wide / build_medium / build_narrow

## Follow-up to `gridwars_run-prw`

The earlier fix scaled `SOFT_LIMIT` in `context_line()` and
`context_line_compact()` via `_effective_soft_limit(ctx)`. But the Builder
that shipped that fix discovered three MORE callsites that still hardcode
`SOFT_LIMIT` in the cost-row "fill" computation:

- `claude/statusline_command.py:2739` (build_wide)
- `claude/statusline_command.py:2786` (build_medium)
- `claude/statusline_command.py:2845` (build_narrow)

Each computes:

    fill = min(total_tokens / SOFT_LIMIT, 1.0)

…then renders a mini-bar in the cost row. On 1M-context Opus those mini-bars
will overflow whenever total session usage crosses 150K — same root cause as
the primary gauge bug, just a smaller visual artifact.

## Fix

Apply the same `_effective_soft_limit(ctx)` swap at all three sites. The
helper already exists at module scope after the earlier fix.

## Acceptance

- All three build functions use `_effective_soft_limit(session.context_window)`
  in place of the bare `SOFT_LIMIT` constant.
- Render with 1M-context model + 170K tokens shows the cost-row mini-bars at
  ~23% fill, not full red.
- Existing tests in `test/` still pass.

## Workflow note

Same as parent bd: apply on `feat/claude-quota-mode`, push to fork. Local-only
workflow; upstream PR is a gift.
close_reason: Fixed: build_wide/medium/narrow now use _effective_soft_limit(); cost-row mini-bars no longer overflow on 1M-context models.

title: [EPIC] Open-Source Polish (README, Contributing, Notices)
description:
# Epic 10 — Open-Source Polish (README, Contributing, Notices)

## Why
GridWars.run is intended to be open source from day one. Contributors and players arriving from GitHub will judge the project on the README. AGPL compliance also demands clean attribution and a contributor pathway. The README isn't just docs — it's the project's storefront.

## Scope
- Top-level `README.md`:
  - Hero line: "GridWars.run — Full PvP. No limits. Free to play. Open source."
  - One-paragraph pitch (cyber-grid, terminal-native, factions, programs, daemons).
  - "Get on the Grid" — local setup: clone w/ submodules, `make install`, `make migrate`, `make run`, connect via telnet/webclient.
  - "What's vendored": brief explanation of `vendor/evennia` and why.
  - License: AGPL-3.0-or-later for GridWars; BSD-3-Clause inside `vendor/evennia` (link THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md).
  - "First playable slice": list of in-game commands (`status`, `scan`, `faction`, `strike`).
  - "Roadmap": brief next-milestone tease (lightcycle duels, daemon AI, sector ownership) — non-binding.
  - Screenshot/asciinema placeholder (TODO with link to follow-up issue).
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`:
  - How to file an issue (link bd or GitHub Issues).
  - PR workflow (branch from main, run `make test`, sign off as required by AGPL/DCO if chosen).
  - Code style (PEP 8, `ruff` if added).
  - "Don't edit vendor/evennia" rule.
- `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` (created in Epic 1, refined here for tone).
- `AGENTS.md`: trim or augment to mention GridWars-specific agent guidance (vs. just bd boilerplate).

## Acceptance
- README renders cleanly on GitHub.
- All commands in setup section work on a fresh clone (verified by Tester or operator).
- CONTRIBUTING.md links to the test command + the "don't touch vendor/" rule.
- License + attribution links resolve.

## Model recommendation
- **Writer (Claude sonnet)** — README voice + pitch.
- **Writer (Claude haiku)** — CONTRIBUTING.md mechanical sections.
- **Reviewer (Claude sonnet)** — final pass for tone, accuracy, broken links.

## Notes
- Depends on Epics 1, 2, and at least one of the gameplay epics shipping so the "first playable slice" section is truthful.
- Can author drafts in parallel; verify-and-ship after gameplay lands.

[Gestalt/codex-dispatch] codex exec backgrounded via Bash run_in_background works well at N=3 parallel on gpt-5.5 — each agent ~1-3min wall, ~50-200k tokens. Pattern: (1) git worktree add .claude/worktrees/codex-<id> -b codex/<id> main, (2) write /tmp/codex-<id>-handoff.md with file targets + constraints + acceptance gate, (3) codex exec --model gpt-5.5 --cd <worktree> --sandbox workspace-write --add-dir /tmp -o /tmp/codex-<id>-finalmsg.md '<short prompt referencing handoff>' > /tmp/codex-<id>-stdout.log 2>&1, (4) on completion: read /tmp/codex-<id>-output.md, commit + push from worktree, gh pr create --body-file (NOT --body HEREDOC — guard rejects). Codex can't push/PR (sandbox limit), Gestalt does that step.
